summary: Implemented ADR 0262 from an isolated worktree by converging the private OpenFGA runtime, reconciling the Keycloak runtime clients used by delegated authorization, hardening the OpenFGA and API gateway live-apply automation, and verifying the delegated authorization graph plus gateway registration end to end before the exact-main merge step.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_openfga_postgres_role.py tests/test_openfga_runtime_role.py tests/test_serverclaw_authz.py tests/test_keycloak_runtime_role.py tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py -q` passed with `42 passed`, and `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_api_gateway_runtime_role.py -q` passed with `4 passed` after the API gateway sync and verification hardening.

`env UV_PYTHON=3.13 make generate-platform-vars`, `make syntax-check-openfga`, `make syntax-check-api-gateway`, `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/validate_repository_data_models.py --validate`, `uv run --with pyyaml python scripts/interface_contracts.py --validate`, `uv run --with pyyaml python scripts/service_completeness.py --validate`, `python3 scripts/container_image_policy.py --validate`, and `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards` all completed successfully on the branch-local live-apply tree.

`make converge-openfga env=production` completed successfully with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 ok=236 changed=7 failed=0`, `localhost ok=2 changed=1 failed=0`, `postgres-lv3 ok=47 changed=0 failed=0`, and `proxmox-host ok=38 changed=7 failed=0`, while the runtime contract pinned the internal HTTP listener to `127.0.0.1:8098` to avoid the existing `browser-runner` use of `8096`.

`curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $(cat /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/openfga/preshared-key.txt)" http://100.64.0.1:8014/stores` returned the `serverclaw-authz` store, and `python3 scripts/serverclaw_authz.py verify --config config/serverclaw-authz/bootstrap.json --openfga-url http://100.64.0.1:8014 --openfga-preshared-key-file /Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.local/openfga/preshared-key.txt --keycloak-url http://10.10.10.20:8091` returned `verification_passed: true` with the declared tuples and checks satisfied.

`make converge-api-gateway env=production` completed successfully after the tree-sync ownership fix and the retried Keycloak verification token request, with final recap `docker-runtime-lv3 ok=242 changed=113 failed=0 skipped=35`.

verification.check: Public gateway verification for OpenFGA registration
verification.observed: `curl -fsS https://api.example.com/healthz` returned `{"status":"ok"}`; the authenticated `https://api.example.com/v1/platform/services` response listed the `openfga` service with `gateway_prefix: /v1/openfga` and internal URL `http://100.64.0.1:8014`; and `https://api.example.com/v1/openfga/healthz` returned the canonical `AUTH_INSUFFICIENT_ROLE` envelope for the `lv3-agent-hub` bearer token, proving the route is live and protected by the expected role gate.

notes: This receipt records the branch-local live apply from `/Users/live/Documents/GITHUB_PROJECTS/platform_server/.worktrees/ws-0262-openfga-keycloak-live-apply` on branch `codex/ws-0262-openfga-keycloak-live-apply` before the exact-main replay and protected-file integration step.
The workstream changed the OpenFGA internal listener from `8096` to `8098` because the separate `browser-runner` runtime already uses `8096` on `docker-runtime-lv3`.
The API gateway sync helper now expands controller-built archives with `tar --no-same-owner --no-same-permissions` so Docker builds on the guest do not inherit controller-specific ownership metadata.
Protected integration files (`VERSION`, `changelog.md`, `README.md`, and `versions/stack.yaml`) intentionally remain pending until the exact-main merge and replay.
